tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine and hyperchloremia, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal color/appearance, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction or uncontrolled diabetes, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, and review of diabetes management and hydration.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
